other_observers: None with me at the time.; Walter Szeliga, Nick Komar, et al. found the bird.
specific_location: Along the Saddle Rock trail, about 100 yards from the parking lot, right where the trail takes a hard left uphill turn away from the creek.
description: Bird was heard only.Behaviors: Bird was not seen, but skulked somewhere inside a dense tangle of underbrush.Call: The bird had a sharp, unmusical, toneless chip, which it often gave singly or in twos and threes, rather like a Wilson's Warbler chip. Recording attached.Plumage: unknown
